options.name: -all
options.description: Removes all path exceptions. This option cannot be used with the -from, -through and -to options

options.name: -exact
options.description: Resets matching exceptions with same number of from|through|to options as specified here

options.name: -fall
options.description: Apply the assertion at the falling edge of the signal on the path endpoints

options.name: -fall_from
options.description: Resets the exceptions from the paths that have falling transition on specified objects (start pins and/or launching clocks)

options.name: -fall_through
options.description: Resets the exceptions from the paths that goes through falling transition of specified pins

options.name: -fall_to
options.description: Resets the exceptions from the paths that have falling transition on specified objects (endpoint pins and/or capturing clocks)

options.name: -from
options.description: Resets the exceptions from the paths that have specified objects (start pins and/or launching clocks)

options.name: -rise
options.description: Applies the assertion at the rising edge of the signal on the path endpoints

options.name: -rise_from
options.description: Resets the exceptions from the paths that have rising transition on specified objects (start pins and/or launching clocks)

options.name: -rise_through
options.description: Resets the exceptions from the paths that goes through rising transition of specified pins

options.name: -rise_to
options.description: Resets the exceptions from the paths that have rising transition on specified objects (endpoint pins and/or capturing clocks)

options.name: -through
options.description: Resets the exceptions from the paths that goes through specified pins

options.name: -to
options.description: Resets the exceptions from the paths that have specified objects (endpoint pins and/or capturing clocks)

options.name: -type
options.description: Removes only path exceptions of the specified type. Valid types are false_path, multicycle and path_delay
